back: A self-referential structure is a data structure (typically a struct or class) that contains a pointer or reference to another structure of the same type.

front: What is the primary purpose of using a self-referential structure?
back: The primary purpose is to create dynamic data structures that can grow and shrink at runtime, such as linked lists, trees, and graphs, by linking nodes of the same type together.

front: Provide a C code example of a self-referential structure for a linked list node.
Why can't a self-referential structure contain an instance of itself (as opposed to a pointer)?
back: It would require infinite storage. If a struct contained an instance of itself, that instance would contain another instance, and so on forever. A pointer provides a finite way to create a reference.

front: Name three common data structures built using self-referential structures.
back: Linked Lists, Binary Trees, and Graphs.
